methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $140M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Sereact is a Series B robotics and manufacturing automation company that has raised $140M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of six same-stage private peers in robotics and manufacturing, supplemented by one public comparable.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $140M total raised as a baseline and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implying a valuation of $266M. The peer-median method benchmarks against six same-sector, same-stage companies—including 1X Technologies ($10.0B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), Collaborative Robotics ($500M), and Fairmat ($350M)—yielding a median valuation of $1.1B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. The wide spread reflects heterogeneity in growth trajectory and market focus within the robotics sector at Series B maturity.

The triangulation of these two methods supports a valuation range of $226M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $670M. The stage-progression approach anchors the lower bound; peer medians anchor the upper bound. The material spread between methods reflects uncertainty regarding which peer cohort most closely mirrors Sereact's technology readiness, go-to-market traction, and addressable market. Narrowing this range would require clarity on revenue run-rate, customer concentration, and competitive positioning within the broader automation sector.
